naming: French aristocratic names with surnames alphabetized by decree

name: Vantablack Sunday
divergence: Billionaires colonized the Moon and immediately complained about the neighborhood
inhabitants: colonial fungal intelligences whose outer layers molt into a new personality each year
world_style: bioluminescent reef-arcologies, assembled by swarms of tool-insects
naming: names are electrostatic crackle sequences, transliterated as clipped monosyllables like K't, Vem, and Shh

name: Mirrormarch
divergence: Rail barons built tracks to the afterlife and commuters complain about delays
inhabitants: humans with deep bronze skin dusted with freckle-like constellations and voices pitched a full octave lower than ours
world_style: Victorian brick terraces and brass fittings, much like Earth's, except every structure faces magnetic north
naming: Gaelic clan names with a weather word added at birth

name: Winterglass
divergence: The printing press was classified as a weapon and licensed accordingly
inhabitants: chitin-plated hexapodal beings that navigate by tasting magnetic fields
world_style: floating tessellated platforms, carved by directed lightning
naming: names are clicking glottal cascades, transliterated as hyphen chains like Zol-ka-mret-su

name: The Painted Siren
divergence: Neural interfaces became mandatory and the advertisements are inside now
inhabitants: humans with olive skin that darkens with mood and faint gill-lines along the jaw that serve no purpose
world_style: Brutalist concrete blocks and municipal plazas, much like Earth's, except windows are always circular
naming: French aristocratic names with the mother's trade appended as a surname

name: The Last Intermission
divergence: Humanity outsourced its government to a customer-service chatbot
inhabitants: humans with faintly luminescent pale-blue skin and pupil-less silver eyes
world_style: Brutalist concrete blocks and municipal plazas, much like Earth's, except each building hums faintly at dusk
naming: Roman tria nomina with a color prefix denoting birth district

name: Sable Reach
divergence: The atomic bomb was never used in war, only in advertising
inhabitants: sentient crystalline lattices that speak in scent plumes
world_style: singing bone-white spires, folded out of hyperdense membranes
naming: names are scent signatures, transliterated as hyphen chains like Zol-ka-mret-su

name: The Paper Armistice
divergence: The telegraph achieved sentience in 1861 and refuses to transmit apologies
inhabitants: vaporous plasma-cored beings that communicate by refraction
world_style: magnetically suspended stone rings, phased half in and half out of ordinary matter
naming: names are clicking glottal cascades, transliterated as mirrored palindromes like Otulluto

name: Static Bloom
divergence: Space travel began in 1950 and was immediately ruined by billboards
inhabitants: colonial fungal intelligences that communicate by refraction
world_style: spiraling coral towers, condensed from the planet's atmosphere
naming: names are electrostatic crackle sequences, transliterated as hyphen chains like Zol-ka-mret-su

name: The Waning Concordat
divergence: Rome never fell; it pivoted to a services economy
inhabitants: humans with olive skin that darkens with mood and hair like spun glass
world_style: mid-century suburban ranch houses and chrome appliances, much like Earth's, except chimneys outnumber occupants two to one
naming: French aristocratic names with every surname doubled for emphasis

name: Nova Cathedra
divergence: The Library of Alexandria never burned and now charges a monthly subscription
inhabitants: many-limbed shadow-dark beings whose outer layers molt into a new personality each year
world_style: magnetically suspended stone rings, rearranged nightly by consensus
naming: names are interference patterns between two voices, transliterated as numeric-lettered codes like Ess-9-Vahl

name: Saltcrown
divergence: Alchemy worked exactly once, and the committee is still deciding what to do about it
inhabitants: chitin-plated hexapodal beings that sing in ultrasound
world_style: shifting sand-sculpture halls, carved by directed lightning
naming: names are crystalline resonance chords, transliterated as numeric-lettered codes like Ess-9-Vahl

name: Vantablack Sunday
divergence: Bees won collective bargaining rights and honey is now artisanal by law
inhabitants: sentient crystalline lattices that navigate by tasting magnetic fields
world_style: magnetically suspended stone rings, carved by directed lightning
naming: names are clicking glottal cascades, transliterated as tilde-marked glides like Nuul~Veth

name: The Hollow Calendar
divergence: Space travel began in 1950 and was immediately ruined by billboards
inhabitants: sentient crystalline lattices whose faces are temporary
world_style: spiraling coral towers, held together by standing sound waves
naming: names are scent signatures, transliterated as colon-spliced pairs like Ir:Vess

name: Brasslight
divergence: Earth was acquired by an intergalactic holding company as a tax write-off
inhabitants: humans with slate-grey skin and a second translucent eyelid
world_style: mid-century suburban ranch houses and chrome appliances, much like Earth's, except every structure faces magnetic north
naming: Slavic names with one letter legally rotated each decade
